title: Pharbitis dillenii
intro: Pharbitis dillenii, also known as Moonflower or Dilleniella, is an annual flowering vine native to tropical and subtropical regions, belonging to the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pharbitis dillenii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pharbitis dillenii include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues.
taxonomy: Pharbitis dillenii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae. It is a member of the genus Pharbitis, which is a group of annual or perennial plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is also known as the Dillenia or Dilleniaceae, although it is not a true Dillenia species. Pharbitis dillenii is a climbing or twining plant with distinctive trumpet-shaped flowers. This classification is based on the plant's morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics.
uses: Pharbitis dillenii, also known as moonflower or ivy-leaved morning glory, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its potential benefits. Its medicinal uses include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of various bioactive compounds. Pharbitis dillenii has been traditionally used in Asian medicine to treat a range of ailments, including coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's therapeutic potential and to validate its traditional uses.

It is used to treat conditions such as fever, headache, and rheumatism due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antiviral properties, making it useful in treating infections and wounds.
